decisions.decision: Page data from last crawl, not WP API
decisions.rationale: Crawl data has audit fields (has_toc, has_schema, has_noindex)

decisions.decision: 4 tab filters with count-badge (Все / Без Schema / Без TOC / Noindex)
decisions.rationale: Focused on audit problems for quick fix
Clean separation from page list, different entity (WpContentJob vs Page)

decisions.decision: HTML diff (green/red) from diff_json for approve review
decisions.rationale: Most informative for mobile, diff_json already exists in WpContentJob
Simple JS pattern, no modals needed
One less step, approve implies intent to push

decisions.decision: TOC/Schema direct push, title/meta through pipeline
decisions.rationale: Risk-based: additive ops safe, content changes need preview

decisions.decision: Title/meta edit on separate screen with SERP preview
decisions.rationale: More space for inputs and preview than inline expand

decisions.decision: 2 bulk ops only: Schema + TOC on all articles
decisions.rationale: No bulk approve in MVP, individual approval sufficient

decisions.decision: Bulk confirmation screen with count + HTMX polling progress bar
decisions.rationale: Phase 29 pattern reuse, Redis counter per page

uncommitted_files_note: Pre-existing modifications unrelated to Phase 31 (crawl feed work from earlier session).
next_action: Run /gsd:ui-phase 31 to generate UI design contract, then /gsd:plan-phase 31. Recommend /clear first.
context_notes: Session completed full Phase 30 lifecycle (plan→execute→verify→complete) and Phase 31 discuss-phase. Phase 31 has rich context (18 decisions, 4 gray areas). All backend infrastructure exists: WpContentJob model, content_pipeline.py, push_to_wp task, mobile router patterns from Phase 26-30. Heavy frontend phase — UI-SPEC recommended before planning.
